food_subgroup: Filled sweet toast / enriched bread pastry

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn term exists for matcha cream filled toast. FOODON_00002683 (bread food product) used as closest parent. A more specific term for filled enriched toast does not exist in the current FoodOn release.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A000E (Bread and similar products) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code exists for matcha cream filled milk toast in the FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226559009 (Bread) used as closest parent. No specific concept for matcha cream filled toast exists in SNOMED CT.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 120 g, representing one piece of matcha cream and red bean filled milk toast as typically served at a Singaporean Chinese pastry shop.

nutrient_source.title: Cream-filled sweet bread / enriched sweet toast — estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for enriched white bread, cream filling, and red bean paste components
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA for matcha cream red bean filled milk toast. Values are estimated by weighted component analysis using USDA SR Legacy entries: enriched white bread (FDC ID 325871), heavy whipping cream (FDC ID 170859), sweetened red bean paste (FDC ID 168591 analogue), butter (FDC ID 173410), and matcha powder (FDC ID 2003571). Approximate composition: ~50% enriched milk bread dough, ~30% matcha cream filling (cream, condensed milk, matcha powder, butter), ~20% red bean paste. Energy and macros are weighted averages. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: White bread GI reference and sweet filled bread analogous estimate — University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for matcha cream red bean filled milk toast. GI estimated at 65 based on the University of Sydney GI Database reference for white bread (GI ~70) and sweet enriched bread products (GI ~60–70). The presence of cream filling, red bean paste, fat, and sugar moderately lowers the effective GI relative to plain white bread. A conservative midpoint of 65 is used. Glycemic load per serving = (65 × 41.5 × 1.20) / 100 = 32.4 g per 120 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~185 mg per 100 g) and butter/condensed milk contributing to overall dietary sodium load
hypertension.guidance: Individuals managing hypertension should limit portion size to one small piece and avoid pairing with other high-sodium foods, as the cumulative sodium from enriched bread dough and dairy ingredients can contribute meaningfully to daily sodium targets of under 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate content (~41.5 g per 100 g), elevated sugar (~16 g per 100 g), and medium GI of 65 producing a high glycemic load per serving (~32 g GL)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this item as an occasional indulgence only; the high glycemic load of approximately 32 per standard serving can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation, and portion control to half a serving with a protein-rich accompaniment is strongly advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat content (~8.8 g per 100 g) from butter, cream, and condensed milk, alongside trans fat (~0.2 g per 100 g) and cholesterol (~48 mg per 100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this pastry due to its saturated fat and cholesterol content; a single 120 g serving provides approximately 10.6 g of saturated fat, approaching or exceeding the recommended daily limit of 20 g for a 2000 kcal diet.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Matcha Cream and Red Bean Toast is a Singaporean Chinese-style enriched milk toast pastry sold at specialty dessert and bread shops such as RUXU (入续 糖水铺与包) in Singapore. The base is a soft, pillowy milk bread (shokupan-style) made from wheat flour, milk, butter, condensed milk, and milk powder, giving it a rich, slightly sweet, and tender crumb. The toast is filled with a luscious stone-ground matcha cream paste made from high-quality matcha powder blended with light cream and condensed milk, delivering a pronounced grassy, umami-tinged bitterness balanced by sweetness. Red bean paste (azuki) adds an earthy, subtly sweet counterpoint and a denser textural contrast to the airy bread. The exterior is typically lightly crisped or toasted, providing a gentle crunch before yielding to the soft interior. Nutritionally, this item is calorie-dense at approximately 318 kcal per 100 g, with significant carbohydrate (41.5 g), fat (14.5 g, of which 8.8 g is saturated), and moderate protein (6.2 g). The sugar content is elevated at 16 g per 100 g due to condensed milk, fine sugar, and red bean paste. The glycemic index is estimated at 65 (medium), and a standard 120 g serving carries a glycemic load of approximately 32, making it a high-GL food. Clinically, this item is best consumed occasionally by individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. It is vegetarian but not vegan or gluten-free. Best consumed on the day of purchase for optimal texture.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for matcha cream red bean filled milk toast exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, FAO/INFOODS SEA, ICMR-NIN). Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component analysis from USDA SR Legacy entries for the constituent ingredients. GI is an analogous estimate based on white bread and sweet enriched bread references from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data (enriched white bread, heavy cream, red bean paste, butter, matcha powder) using an approximate composition of 50% milk bread dough, 30% matcha cream filling, and 20% red bean paste. All micronutrient fields with values are derived from this weighted estimation; fields reported as -1 indicate the nutrient was not reliably estimable from available component data or was not reported in the source entries used. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are all estimated from component USDA SR Legacy data and carry medium uncertainty. No sentinel -1 values are present in per_100g; all fields have been estimated from component data. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database white bread and sweet bread references; direct measurement for this specific product is not available. Halal status is unconfirmed as the restaurant's certification status is not publicly verified. The food is best consumed on the day of purchase per the vendor's own notes.

improvement_actions_taken: Constructed full multilingual local_names for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi to satisfy G6 for Singaporean Chinese cuisine.
Estimated per_100g nutrient values via weighted component analysis from USDA SR Legacy entries, as no exact source entry exists.
Assigned GI of 65 as analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database white bread and sweet bread references.
Selected closest_parent ontology codes for FoodOn, FoodEx2, and SNOMED CT with honest gap notes, as no exact codes exist for this specific product.
Ensured all aliases_by_language entries appear verbatim in the flat aliases list.
Documented all estimation methodology and source limitations in notes and nutrient_source fields.
Set human_condition_name to 'Type 2 Diabetes' and machine key to type2_diabetes per project rules.
